achievements.hierarchy_hero: Title for completing hierarchy tasks. Represents mastering note hierarchies, NOT being an aggressive ruler or dominator.
achievements.secret_publisher: Title for sharing cards. Represents sharing notes securely, NOT covert/suspicious behavior.
auth.agree_terms: Prepended to the Terms & Privacy links (e.g., 'I agree to the [Terms]...'). Ensure the translation bridges grammatically to trailing links (like adding a colon for SOV languages).
datetime.abbr_hour: Used for relative durations (e.g. '3h ago'). Represents hours elapsed, NOT a specific o'clock time (like 3 o'clock).
datetime.abbr_month: Used for relative durations (e.g. '2mo ago'). Represents months elapsed, NOT the calendar name of a month (like January).
editor.escaping: Progressive status text shown in the editor when the user holds down the Escape key to cancel editing or exit. NOT for escaping formatting characters (like backslashes).
print_import_export.print_options.reveal: Option to reveal hidden spoiler text (unblur). NOT to blur text.
quick_tips.edit_mode_instructions: Instructions on entering edit mode. The Enter key should be translated as the keyboard key Enter/Return, NOT the verb 'to type/input'.
quick_tips.mobile_parent_instructions: Mobile guide instructions explaining card parent-child hierarchies. Avoid biological and folder terminology.
quick_tips.parent_instructions: Guide instructions explaining note hierarchies. Note that 'parent' and 'child' refer to structural note relationships, NOT biological family relatives or traditional filesystem folders.
subscription.seamless_offline: Indicates the app's capability to work smoothly while offline. NOT related to a poor or unstable network connection.
subscription.supporting_small_business: A supportive message thanking users for supporting an independent development team. NOT financial aid, customer support, or public welfare.
universal_search.button: Represents a button component UI label. NOT a physical keyboard or piano key.
user.never: Used as a status placeholder meaning 'never'. Note: Do NOT use this as a button label for canceling confirmation dialogs, as it translates to 'None' in some languages.
user_limit.junk_cards_free_space: Choice to throw away cards to clear quota space. Releasing cards to the recycle bin/trash, NOT deleting them permanently.
user_preferences_behavior.quick_couple_k: The shortcut key used to trigger the Coupler linking tool. 'Couple' means to link or connect cards, NOT romantic partners.
actions.junk: Verb/button label for moving a card to the trash/recycle bin, shown next to Pin/Unpin actions. NOT related to junk mail or spam.
cheatsheet.junk_card: Keyboard-shortcut cheatsheet label for moving a card to the trash/recycle bin. Same meaning as 'Junk' elsewhere, NOT junk mail or spam.
actions.pin: Bookmarks a card to a dedicated 'Pinned' sidebar pane for quick access (thumbtack icon), like pinning a message or browser tab. NOT a PIN security code/passcode.
pinned.unpin_all: Removes all cards from the 'Pinned' sidebar pane at once. 'Pin' refers to bookmarking, NOT a PIN security code.
cards.link: Noun for a card-to-card hyperlink reference, used in phrases like '+3 links from an external card' in the Backlinks panel. NOT the verb 'to link' or a generic web URL.
cards.links: Plural of 'link' as a card-to-card hyperlink reference (see cards.link). NOT the verb 'to link'.
editor.spoiler: Editor text-formatting feature (alongside Bold/Highlight/Strikethrough) that blurs/hides text until the reader hovers or taps to reveal it (markdown syntax `!!text!!`). NOT a narrative plot-spoiler warning and NOT a car spoiler.
sizing.smart_sized: Card display-size option between Minimized and Maximized, showing an adaptively-truncated content summary rather than the full card. 'Smart' means automatic/adaptive sizing, NOT an AI feature.
superpowers.title: Brand name for the app's AI-assisted features, always styled as '**super**powers' with 'super' bolded. This same word is also reused as a rechargeable usage-credit mechanic that gates AI features (see errors.out_superpowers/wait_recharge) — NOT literal comic-book superpowers.
superpowers.powers: The non-bolded suffix of the 'superpowers' brand name (bold_super + powers = 'superpowers'). Also doubles as the plain noun for the app's rechargeable AI usage-credits (e.g. 'wait for your powers to recharge'). NOT literal magical powers.
superpowers.bold_super: The bolded prefix of the 'superpowers' brand name (bold_super + powers = '**super**powers'), rendered in a heavier font weight. Keep it as a prefix that combines directly with 'powers', NOT a standalone word meaning 'great/excellent'.
errors.out_superpowers: Error shown when a user has exhausted their rechargeable AI-usage credits (the 'Superpowers' quota), not a general power/electricity outage.
errors.wait_recharge: Tells the user to wait for their AI-usage credits ('Superpowers') to replenish over time. 'Recharge' is a quota/credits metaphor, NOT a literal battery or device charging.
visibility.priority: Highest card-visibility tier: card is fully visible AND shown in the Outline tree ('Visible + Shown in Outline'). Refers to display prominence, NOT task/todo urgency ranking.
visibility.invisible: Lowest card-visibility tier: card is hidden from the Noteboard/Search by default and only appears via explicit filtering ('Usually hidden, requires filtering'). Refers to a display-visibility setting, NOT account privacy or being hidden from other users.
sort.manual: Sort option meaning the user's own custom drag-and-drop card ordering is preserved, as opposed to sorting by date, name, etc. NOT a reference to an instruction manual.
user.scope_append: API key permission-scope label meaning the key can create cards and comments (in addition to reading). Part of a tiered permission system, NOT related to variable/code 'scope'.
user.scope_full: API key permission-scope label meaning full read/write API access. Part of a tiered permission system for developer API keys.
user.scope_legacy: Label for an API key created before the current permission-scope tier system existed. Means 'predates this system', NOT 'outdated/deprecated software' in general.
user.scope_read: API key permission-scope label meaning read-only access, the lowest tier in the API key permission system.
flashcards.again: Spaced-repetition review-rating button (FSRS/Anki-style) meaning 'I forgot this card' — schedules it to reappear soon. NOT a general 'repeat' or 'again' action.
flashcards.good: Spaced-repetition review-rating button (FSRS/Anki-style) meaning 'I recalled this with normal effort'. NOT a general quality/mood descriptor.
flashcards.hard: Spaced-repetition review-rating button (FSRS/Anki-style) meaning 'I recalled this but with difficulty'. NOT a general difficulty descriptor.
flashcards.easy: Spaced-repetition review-rating button (FSRS/Anki-style) meaning 'I recalled this with no effort'. NOT a general difficulty descriptor.
depth.infinite_depth: Option in the 'View Depth' menu that expands all levels of nested child cards in a hierarchical view (e.g. Outline). 'Depth' refers to hierarchy nesting, NOT physical depth or z-index.
depth.one_layer: Option in the 'View Depth' menu showing only one level of nested child cards. 'Layer' refers to a hierarchy nesting level, NOT physical/visual layering.
depth.two_layers: Option in the 'View Depth' menu showing two levels of nested child cards. 'Layer' refers to hierarchy nesting levels, NOT physical/visual layering.
cheatsheet.magic_paste: Default paste shortcut (Cmd/Ctrl+V) that preserves/converts rich formatting when pasting content into a card. Contrasts with 'Plain paste' (strips formatting). 'Magic' is a marketing flourish, NOT a magic-trick/fantasy theme.
cheatsheet.plain_paste: Paste shortcut (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+V) that strips formatting and pastes as plain text, contrasting with 'Magic paste'.
universal_coupler.appending: Status label in the Coupler linking tool indicating content/cards being moved will be inserted at the END of the target card's list. 'Coupler' means linking/connecting cards, NOT a romantic pairing or mechanical coupling.
universal_coupler.prepending: Status label in the Coupler linking tool indicating content/cards being moved will be inserted at the START of the target card's list. 'Coupler' means linking/connecting cards, NOT a romantic pairing or mechanical coupling.
user_preferences_behavior.clickbindings: Preference section for customizing mouse click behavior (e.g. swapping which of single-click/double-click opens a card). A deliberate coinage meaning 'click-based bindings', NOT a typo for 'keybindings'.
common_terms.seamless: Reused word fragment combined with other strings (e.g. + 'Mode' = 'Seamless Mode', a distraction-free continuous reading view). In this reading-mode context it describes an uninterrupted UI experience, NOT network/offline reliability (see subscription.seamless_offline for that other sense).
editor.quick_tip_parents: First of four concatenated fragments forming a sentence: '[parents] and [card links] to connect them.' Must grammatically fit as a noun before 'and', referring to parent-card hierarchy.
editor.quick_tip_and: Second of four concatenated fragments forming a sentence: '[parents] and [card links] to connect them.' Must grammatically fit between two noun phrases; reorder if the target language needs different word order.
editor.quick_tip_card_links: Third of four concatenated fragments forming a sentence: '[parents] and [card links] to connect them.' Refers to card-to-card hyperlinks, must grammatically fit as a noun after 'and'.
editor.quick_tip_to_connect: Fourth of four concatenated fragments forming a sentence: '[parents] and [card links] to connect them.' Trailing clause referring back to both preceding nouns.
menu.enter_to: Prefix fragment immediately followed by a button label, forming phrases like 'Enter to Save'. 'Enter' is the keyboard key, NOT the verb 'to enter/input data'.
menu.esc_to: Prefix fragment immediately followed by a button label, forming phrases like 'Esc to Cancel'. 'Esc' is the keyboard key, NOT a verb.
